a:r.a:t: Vessel Taxonomy | MRO TAM scope covers every SECNAV and USCG hull category
Any commissioned ship built or armed for naval combat.
Ships with the capability to contribute directly to Navy warfighting or directly to other combatant operations and support missions.
Any naval ship designed to operate in the open ocean in a variety of sea states to provide indirect support to combatant forces or services to shore-based establishments and infrastructure.

Smaller vessels designed to meet various combat-related mission roles, including amphibious warfare, insertion, patrol, overwatch and enemy denial-of-use, and mobility of riverine and littoral areas. Combatant craft are not part of the battle force inventory.
Unmanned maritime vessels and vehicles are platforms designed to operate remotely, independently or integrated with manned platforms. Certain unmanned maritime vehicles may, in the future, be part
Support craft are noncommissioned vessels and watercraft designed to provide support for naval operations or shore-based establishments and are not part of the battle force inventory.
Any commissioned vessel 65’ in length or greater, with accommodations for a crew to live aboard.
Any vessel under 65’ in length without a permanently assigned crew.
Amphibious Warfare Ships (LHA / LHD / LPD / LSD)
Combat Logistics Ships (AO / AOE / AOL / AKE)
Salvage Ships & Fleet Ocean Tugs (ARS / ATF / ATS)
Research, Survey & Instrumentation Ships (AGM / AGOR / AGS)
Special Warfare Craft (DSB / CCA / CCM / CCH / NSW RHIB / SDV / SWCS / SSC)
Unmanned Undersea Vehicles (XLUUV / LUUV / MUUV / SUUV)
Lighters & Barges (YC / YCV / YFN / YFNX / YFNB / YON / YOS / YWN / YWO)
Other Craft, Self-Propelled (FSF / SBX / YFB / YP / YSD / YDT / YTT)
Other Craft, Non-Self-Propelled (APL / YD / YFND / YFP / YR / YRB / YRBM / YRDH / YRDM / IX)
Sealift Support Craft (INLS / LCM 8 / LARC V / MPF UB / OPDS UB)
Response Boats (MLB / RB-M / TPSB / RB-S II / UTM / UTL)
Aids to Navigation Boats (ANB / BUSL / TANB / AB-S / AB-SKF)
Cutter Boats (CB-LRI / CB-OTH / ASB/LC / MSB / CB-L / CB-ATON / CB-M / CB-S / UTL)
Special Purpose Craft (SPC-SV / SPC-TTB / SPC-TB / SPC-LE / SPC-BTD / SPC-LEO / SPC-SW / SPC-IRT / SKF-ICE / SKF)
Military Sealift Command (MSC) vessels sit across Combatant Ships and Auxiliary Ships
Boats of the United States Coast Guard (2024)
U.S. Navy and Coast Guard Vessel Taxonomy
